{"query":"Intel Foundry Secure Enclave CHIPS Act funding amount announcement trusted U.S. semiconductor manufacturing","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1368,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.intc.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/1718/intel-and-biden-harris-administration-finalize-7-86","title":"Intel and Biden-Harris Administration Finalize $7.86 Billion Funding Award Under US CHIPS Act :: Intel Corporation (INTC)","content":"Department of Commerce has awarded Intel up to $7.86 billion in direct funding through the U.S.
CHIPS and Science Act to advance Intel’s commercial semiconductor manufacturing and advanced packaging projects in Arizona, New Mexico, Ohio and Oregon.
Today’s award, coupled with a 25% investment tax credit, will support Intel’s plans to invest more than $100 billion in the U.S.
SANTA CLARA, Calif.--(BUSINESS WIRE)-- Intel Corporation and the Biden-Harris Administration announced today that the U.S.
A September 2024 photo shows a powerful crane on the Intel Ohio One construction site in Licking County, Ohio.
The announcement demonstrates the U.S. government’s confidence in Intel’s essential role in building a resilient, trusted semiconductor supply chain on domestic soil.
Intel was founded in the U.S. and has been innovating, investing and supporting global semiconductor manufacturing and R&D for more than 50 years.
In September 2024, Intel won a manufacturing contract for up to $3 billion for the Secure Enclave program.
Alongside its manufacturing and technology investments, Intel has a long-standing history of investing in the American workforce by supporting education, training and benefits programs needed to create the jobs of the future.
As part of Intel’s overall CHIPS award, $65 million is set aside to support the company’s efforts to create a more skilled semiconductor workforce."},{"url":"https://www.semiconductors.org/chip-supply-chain-investments/","title":"Semiconductor Supply Chain Investments - Semiconductor Industry Association","content":"Technology: 180nm and 350nm analog

Additional Information: “The proposed CHIPS funding would support the expansion and modernization of MACOM’s existing facility which would increase the production of 100mm Gallium Nitride (GaN) and Gallium Arsenide (GaAs) semiconductor fabrication and introduce the production of 150mm GaN.”
Bosch expects to produce its first chips on 200-millimeter wafers in its Roseville facility starting in 2026.
Incentive Amount: $16 million
Technology: 90nm and 130nm foundry
Additional Information: “The Biden-Harris Administration’s proposed investment of up to $33 million would support the modernization and expansion of a state-of-the-art manufacturing cleanroom in Coherent’s existing 700,000 square-foot facility in Sherman, Texas to establish the world’s first 150mm indium phosphide (InP) manufacturing line by adding advanced wafer fabrication equipment to produce InP devices at scale.
With this proposed investment, the Biden-Harris Administration would be supporting a reliable, domestic supply of MEMS devices manufactured on 300mm wafers, further strengthening U.S. supply chain resilience while creating over 75 jobs in the state of Florida.”
Additional Information: “This proposed funding would catalyze investment from private and state sources to expand Polar’s manufacturing facility and introduce new technology capabilities in Bloomington, Minnesota.

The $7.86 billion CHIPS Act funding will be used to support Intel's semiconductor manufacturing and advanced packaging projects in Arizona (where Intel's most advanced Fab 52 and Fab 62 are located), New Mexico (where Intel's most advanced packaging facilities are), Ohio (home for Intel's future fabs), and Oregon (the company's major hub for semiconductor R&D). In addition to $7.86 billion from the U.S. Department of Commerce, Intel is also set to get $3 billion from the U.S. Department of Defense to make chips for the U.S. government, intelligence and military agencies under the Secure Enclave program.","score":0.8190992,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.manufacturingdive.com/news/intel-3-billion-award-secure-enclave-national-security-defense-chips-act/727163","title":"Intel wins $3B to produce chips for US defense","content":"Let Manufacturing Dive’s free newsletter keep you informed, straight from your inbox. - [x]  By signing up to receive our newsletter, you agree to our Terms of Use and Privacy Policy. # Intel wins $3B to produce chips for US defense. The funding is Intel’s second award under the CHIPS Act and aims to boost the government’s supply of leading-edge semiconductors. *   Intel was awarded up to $3 billion under the CHIPS and Science Act on Monday to manufacture leading-edge semiconductors for the Department of Defense. *   The award is Intel’s second under the CHIPS Act, and builds on previous projects between the Defense Department and the company to produce advanced chips. *   The announcement is separate from the $8.5 billion CHIPS funding Intel received in March to support semiconductor manufacturing at sites in Arizona, New Mexico, Ohio and Oregon. While the CHIPS Act funding program is administered by the Commerce Department, the Defense Department will execute the funding of the Secure Enclave award, according to a joint statement from the two agencies Monday.","score":0.8047902,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.enr.com/articles/59941-biden-harris-administration-finalizes-79b-in-chips-act-funding-for-intel","title":"Biden-Harris Administration Finalizes $7.9B in CHIPS Act Funding for Intel | Engineering News-Record","content":"Biden-Harris Administration Finalizes $7.9B in CHIPS Act Funding for Intel | Engineering News-Record. # Biden-Harris Administration Finalizes $7.9B in CHIPS Act Funding for Intel. of Commerce announced Nov. 26 it has finalized up to $7.86 billion in direct funding from the CHIPS and Science Act for Intel's commercial semiconductor manufacturing and packaging projects under construction in Arizona, New Mexico, Ohio and Oregon. “The CHIPS for America program will supercharge American innovation and technology and make our country more secure—and Intel is playing an important role in the revitalization of the U.S. semiconductor industry through its unprecedented investments across Arizona, New Mexico, Ohio and Oregon,” said U.S. Secretary of Commerce Gina Raimondo, in a statement. “Thanks to the leadership of President Biden and Vice President Harris, our CHIPS award is enabling Intel to drive one of the most significant semiconductor manufacturing expansions in U.S. history.”.","score":0.7608822,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.investing.com/news/company-news/intel-secures-3-billion-for-chip-security-program-93CH-3618203","title":"Intel secures $3 billion for chip security program","content":"SANTA CLARA, Calif. - Intel Corporation (NASDAQ:INTC) has been awarded up to $3 billion in funding from the U.S. government under the CHIPS","score":0.7295395,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://newsroom.intel.com/press-kit/us-chips-act-funding-intel","title":"Press Kit: US CHIPS Act Funding for Intel - Newsroom","content":"A crane supports the structure, and other cranes are visible in the background against a clear blue sky. ## Award supports Intel’s plans to advance American semiconductor manufacturing and technology leadership. On Nov. 26, 2024, Intel Corporation and the U.S. Department of Commerce announced an agreement to award the company $7.86 billion in direct funding for its commercial semiconductor manufacturing projects under the U.S. CHIPS and Science Act. This announcement follows the preliminary memorandum of terms agreement announced March 20, 2024. Funding will help advance Intel’s critical semiconductor manufacturing and advanced packaging projects at its sites in Arizona, New Mexico, Ohio and Oregon – U.S. locations where the company produces some of the world’s most advanced chips and semiconductor packaging technologies. Together, CHIPS Act funding and Intel’s previously announced plans to invest more than $100 billion in the U.S. constitutes one of the largest public-private investments in the U.S. semiconductor industry.","score":0.725824,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":1.35,"request_id":"1637cac7-2d75-4192-b650-2a17846a3d2e"}
